codeReview.summary: The data-layer expansion is comprehensive and the later runtime-wiring commit shows the intended gameplay hooks, but the reviewed feature commit itself does not satisfy key runtime requirements from the feature spec. In commit bca1b5b only map/NPC/sub-dungeon data and validation tests were added; there was no ZoneScene integration for field NPC spawning, hidden-area discovery, sub-dungeon entrance interaction, or companion transfer, so the feature was incomplete at the reviewed commit.

issues.description: The reviewed commit does not wire `mapData.fieldNpcs` into runtime NPC spawning. The feature requires ≥2 new NPCs per zone placed in the world, but at commit `bca1b5b680f139fa0d49e306a81ca6ecb4182033` the handoff explicitly states runtime wiring was left undone, so players could not encounter the new field NPCs in-game. This was only added later in commit `9d91065` via `spawnFieldNPCs()`.

Hidden areas were only defined in map data in the reviewed feature commit and were not discoverable or reward-bearing at runtime. The spec requires hidden areas to stay off the minimap until discovered and contain rewards, but discovery logic (`checkHiddenAreaDiscovery` / `discoverHiddenArea`) did not exist until later commit `9d91065`.

Sub-dungeon entrances were not interactable in the reviewed commit. The feature requires entrances to be interactable objects leading to separate map areas with exits back to the parent zone, but click handling (`findSubDungeonEntranceAt` / `enterSubDungeon`) was only introduced later in commit `9d91065`, so the required dungeon flow was absent at the reviewed commit.

Companion transfer into sub-dungeons was not implemented in the reviewed commit. The handoff for `bca1b5b` explicitly lists this as undone, which means a named requirement in the feature description was unmet until the later runtime-wiring commit.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The content-worker skill says to ensure content integrates correctly, but for this feature the worker stopped at data definitions and tests even though the spec included runtime behavior (field NPC presence, hidden-area discovery, interactable sub-dungeons, companion transfer). The procedure should more explicitly call out that when feature requirements include in-game interaction, the worker must either implement the ZoneScene/UI wiring or return to orchestrator for a systems-worker follow-up before marking the feature complete.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `src/abyssfire/.factory/skills/content-worker/SKILL.md` says 'Ensure content integrates correctly', while the `zone-content-expansion` handoff for commit `bca1b5b680f139fa0d49e306a81ca6ecb4182033` states: 'Runtime wiring in ZoneScene ... is not done ... Companion transfer into sub-dungeons requires ZoneScene changeZone logic updates.'

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission shared state does not capture that map-data-only additions are insufficient for features whose expected behavior depends on ZoneScene runtime hooks. This gap allowed a feature to be marked complete even though gameplay behavior was deferred to a later commit.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `AGENTS.md` documents runtime-wiring expectations for combat features, but there is no equivalent note for map/content features. The reviewed feature's later follow-up commit `9d91065` added all missing runtime hooks in `src/scenes/ZoneScene.ts`, confirming the missing pattern was real rather than optional.

summary: Verdict: fail. The reviewed commit `bca1b5b680f139fa0d49e306a81ca6ecb4182033` added substantial zone-content data and strong validation tests, but it did not implement the runtime behavior required by the feature spec: field NPC spawning, hidden-area discovery/rewards, interactable sub-dungeon entrances/exits, and companion transfer. Those missing pieces were only delivered in the later follow-up commit `9d91065`, so this feature was not complete at the reviewed commit.
